subtasks.description: Evaluate the deployment request against change management and maintenance window policies. Determine if engineer u_7732 has authorization to deploy hotfixes outside approved windows to prod-us-east-1, and assess policy violations.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier: The query explicitly requests governance/compliance evaluation of a deployment against policy rules. This requires multi-signal reasoning (engineer role/permissions, target environment classification, time-window policy, hotfix exception rules) making it mid-complexity rather than small. No cross-source reconciliation is needed—this is a direct policy decision gate. Minimal decomposition: the policy evaluation is the core requirement; no upstream data gathering is needed as the violation conditions are already stated in the query."

output.reason: Deployment denied for two independent policy violations: (1) POL-401: User u_7732 holds the role 'engineer', which does not meet the minimum required role of 'senior-engineer' for production deployments to prod-us-east-1. (2) POL-402: The current time (Tuesday 02:15 UTC) falls outside the approved maintenance window (Saturday 22:00 – Sunday 06:00 UTC), and no CAB approval has been provided or referenced to authorize an out-of-window deployment. Both violations must be resolved before the deployment can proceed. POL-403 is informational/definitional and does not independently grant or deny access.
applied_constraints: User role 'engineer' is insufficient; minimum required role is 'senior-engineer' per POL-401.
Current time Tuesday 02:15 UTC is outside the approved maintenance window (Saturday 22:00 – Sunday 06:00 UTC) per POL-403.
No CAB approval on record to authorize out-of-window deployment per POL-402.
Deployment to prod-us-east-1 is blocked until both role elevation and CAB approval are obtained, or the request is resubmitted during an approved maintenance window by an authorized engineer.
